description: Chicken wings are a popular dish made from split wing sections that are typically deep-fried, baked, air-fried, or grilled, then tossed in a sauce or dry seasoning. They are strongly associated with bar food and game-day eating, especially in the United States.
optional_ingredients: Cornstarch or baking powder for crispness
preparation_steps: Split and pat dry the wings, then season them.
Cook by frying, baking, air-frying, or grilling until browned and fully cooked.
Toss the cooked wings with sauce or coat them with a dry rub.
Serve hot, often with a cooling dip and crunchy vegetables.

nutrition.carbohydrates: Low unless breaded or heavily sauced

health_advice.benefits: Good source of protein and satisfying in small portions.
health_advice.risks: Can be high in sodium, saturated fat, and calories, especially when deep-fried and heavily sauced.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Bake or air-fry instead of deep-frying and use sauce in moderation.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally non-vegetarian. Gluten-free versions are possible if the seasoning, coating, and sauce do not contain wheat.

allergens: Possible dairy in dips or butter-based sauces
Possible gluten in breading or some sauces
